name: Milwaukee Pork Stew
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, mix salt, pepper and flour.
2. Dredge each piece of pork in the flour mixture.
3. Heat oil in a deep skillet over medium heat.
4. Add pork to the skillet and brown on all sides.
5. Remove onions and garlic with a slotted spoon and set aside.
6. Cook 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until pork is browned.
7. Pour off any excess drippings from the pork.
8. Stir in parsley, caraway seed, remaining onions, chicken stock, beer, vinegar, and brown sugar.
9. Cover and simmer for 1 1/4 hours, stirring occasionally, or until pork is very tender.
Serving Suggestion: Serve with buttered noodles, rye bread, and cabbage slaw.
